title: tragopogon cazorlanus
intro: Tragopogon cazorlanus, also known as Cazorla Salsify, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula. This biennial plant, belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae), has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Tragopogon cazorlanus are attributed to its ability to stimulate digestion, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health concerns. Herbal preparations of Tragopogon cazorlanus are typically made from its roots and leaves, and are often used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
